methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Julien Dereux that has raised €100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 firms across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and General Matter.

The peer-median method is not applicable in this instance. The nuclear energy sector contains only four same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable triangulation. A filtered analysis by stage, market tier, and capital structure yields insufficient depth; applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise and obscure sector-specific dynamics. The limited density of Series A nuclear technology companies at similar capital deployment stages constrains statistical precision.

Without sufficient narrow peer coverage, this analysis cannot support a single point valuation or narrow range. The data indicates that Calogena should be benchmarked against the broader nuclear energy comp set qualitatively, but quantitative valuation synthesis requires either: additional Series A nuclear peers with disclosed metrics; revenue or cash-burn multiples from the identified public or private firms; or disclosed recent funding rounds at known valuations. The key uncertainty is the absence of a dense, stage-aligned comparable set needed to anchor a defensible valuation range.
